modules.title: Content engineering with AI
modules.subtitle: Images, video, and audio
modules.description: Learn to create images, short videos, and audio with AI – from brief to quality checks before publishing.

content.whyBenefit: After this module you will know how to create images, short videos and audio with AI – from pipeline and prompts to consistency, tools and quality checks.
content.heroSubText: For marketing and communication specialists – visual and audio content with AI.
content.firstActionCTA: Start with the quick path: create one hero image with the prompt you used.
outcomes: Pipeline: brief → stills → refs → I2V → audio → edit → QA
Business: KPIs, risks, C2PA/disclosure, path from brief to publication

short.label: Short path
short.description: Skip optional slides (composition, MASTER templates, glossary).

slides.subtitle: Images, video, music – tools and prompts

sections.body: Connect the campaign goal to the right visual choice: awareness, engagement or conversion.

slides.title: Generative media pipeline
slides.subtitle: Brief → stills → refs → I2V → audio → edit → QA

sections.heading: In short
sections.body: In 2026 practice, the chain wins – not the “best model”. One prompt into a video generator means expensive retries. Pipeline: brief + brand → stills/storyboard → reference lock → short 3–5 s I2V clips → audio (audio-first) → edit → QA and provenance.

sections.heading: Pipeline diagram
sections.body: Six steps – tap a stage in the diagram. For the business cycle (brief → A/B), see the slide “Workflow: from brief to publication”.
sections.imageAlt: Generative media pipeline: brief, stills, refs, I2V, audio, QA

sections.heading: How to read the diagram
sections.body: The six diagram steps are the technical backbone. The checklist below is practice for your topic – not a second step list.

sections.heading: Do this now
sections.body: Fill in the checklist for your topic – this is a plan, not a generator prompt.

sections.heading: Copyable template
sections.body: Pipeline checklist – copy and fill in.
sections.copyable:
Pipeline checklist:
Brief: goal [Awareness/Engagement/Conversion], audience [who], platform [where].
Brand: colours [X], tone [Y].
Stills: hero + [0–2] extra frames (lock before video).
Refs: [product/character – 3–5 angles] / none.
Clips: [2–4] × 3–5 s (I2V), not one long one-shot.
Audio: [VO first / bed only] + rights [licensed / demo].
Edit: cut + grade + mix.
QA: brand | message | format | rights | disclosure (C2PA/label).

sections.heading: Check
sections.body: Do you have at least one locked still before video? Do you know whether audio will be VO-first or bed only?

sections.heading: Where to use this
sections.body: Ad clips, social Reels, product demos, internal explainers.

slides.title: Image generation
slides.subtitle: Section: prompts, style, ratios and tools

content.title: Image generation
content.subtitle: Next: image prompts (subject + context + style), ratios, brand, consistency. Tools: FLUX, GPT-Image, Midjourney, Ideogram.

slides.title: Image prompt basics
slides.subtitle: What to describe: subject, style, ratio

sections.heading: In short
sections.body: A good image prompt describes the subject, context, style, ratio and what to avoid.

sections.heading: Formula and three layers
sections.body: A good image prompt describes the subject, context, style, ratio and what to avoid.

sections.heading: Minimum requirements
sections.body: A good image prompt describes the subject, context, style, ratio and what to avoid.

sections.heading: Do this now
sections.body: A good image prompt describes the subject, context, style, ratio and what to avoid.

sections.heading: Copyable prompt
sections.body: A good image prompt describes the subject, context, style, ratio and what to avoid.
sections.copyable: Create an image: [DESCRIPTION]. Style: professional, bright, minimal. Ratio: 16:9. Do not add text inside the image.

sections.heading: Check
sections.body: A good image prompt describes the subject, context, style, ratio and what to avoid.

sections.heading: Why this works
sections.body: A good image prompt describes the subject, context, style, ratio and what to avoid.

slides.title: Style and ratios for images
slides.subtitle: Style controls look; ratio controls format

sections.heading: In short
sections.body: Use style, ratio and brand rules so the image does not look random.

sections.heading: Brand consistency
sections.body: Use style, ratio and brand rules so the image does not look random.

sections.heading: Do this now
sections.body: Use style, ratio and brand rules so the image does not look random.

sections.heading: Copyable prompt
sections.body: Use style, ratio and brand rules so the image does not look random.
sections.copyable: Image: [WHAT IS SHOWN]. Style: [photo / vector / 3D]. Ratio: [1:1 / 16:9 / 9:16]. Use a neutral scene and avoid text unless needed.

sections.heading: Check
sections.body: Use style, ratio and brand rules so the image does not look random.

sections.heading: Tools
sections.body: Use style, ratio and brand rules so the image does not look random.

sections.heading: Which tool for which case
sections.body: GPT-Image / ChatGPT – natural language, fast brief→image. FLUX – photorealism and multi-reference consistency. Midjourney – high artistic level, character ref. Leonardo.ai – photorealism, product design. Ideogram – text in images (logo, posters). Adobe Firefly – CC, legally safer sources, C2PA. Google Imagen – object retention, SynthID – safe advertising. Stable Diffusion – open, flexible (advanced).

slides.title: Quick check: style and ratios
slides.subtitle: 3 questions before composition and the builder

questions.question: What should you specify for a square social post image?
options: The 1:1 ratio
Only the tool name
Only the word “beautiful”
Only the music mood
questions.explanation: The best answer uses a clear goal, context, format and quality check.

questions.question: Which field controls the image look the most?
options: Style, such as photorealistic, 3D or minimalist vector graphics
The downloaded file name
The number of test questions
The module duration
questions.explanation: The best answer uses a clear goal, context, format and quality check.

questions.question: How do you reduce a random, inconsistent brand look?
options: Specify colours, tone, typography direction and a clean visual identity
Keep the prompt as generic as possible
Specify only the duration in seconds
Use only a music tool
questions.explanation: The best answer uses a clear goal, context, format and quality check.

sections.heading: In short
sections.body: A prompt alone does not lock identity. In marketing you need reference lock: 3–5 reference images (different angles) and the rule “same product / same character / same style / same color palette”. Without that, the campaign set “drifts”.

sections.heading: Reference lock – diagram
sections.body: Four steps – tap a stage. Checklist and CopyButton below.
sections.imageAlt: Reference lock: refs, rule, generate, QA

sections.heading: Brand / product sheet (minimum)
sections.body: (1) Hero / front view. (2) ¾ or side. (3) Flatlay or detail (label). (4) Optional – style / lighting ref.

sections.heading: Do this now
sections.body: Collect or generate 3 references for your product or character. Copy the rule into your next generation.

sections.heading: Copyable template
sections.body: Reference lock rule.
sections.copyable:
Reference lock: use the same 3–5 reference images.
Rule: same product, same proportions, same label/logo placement, same color palette, same style.
New scene: [SETTING / ACTION]. Camera: [angle]. Format: [1:1 / 16:9 / 9:16].
No text in the image (unless label on product).

sections.heading: Check
sections.body: Did the product distort, change colour or lose its label? If yes – simplify the scene, strengthen refs or inpaint only the problem area. Avoid real people’s faces without consent.

slides.title: Composition and framing
slides.subtitle: Rule of thirds, camera angle, shot types

sections.heading: In short
sections.body: Composition and camera language help you control what the viewer sees first.

sections.heading: Rule-of-thirds grid
sections.body: Composition and camera language help you control what the viewer sees first.

sections.heading: Rule of thirds and planes
sections.body: Composition and camera language help you control what the viewer sees first.

sections.heading: Camera angle and shot types
sections.body: Composition and camera language help you control what the viewer sees first.

sections.heading: Do this now
sections.body: Composition and camera language help you control what the viewer sees first.

sections.heading: Copyable template: composition and framing
sections.body: Composition and camera language help you control what the viewer sees first.
sections.copyable: Image: [SUBJECT and ACTION]. Setting: [CONTEXT]. Composition: rule of thirds, subject on the right intersection. Camera: [close-up / medium / wide]. Style: [STYLE]. Ratio: 16:9.

sections.heading: Narrative image (optional)
sections.body: Composition and camera language help you control what the viewer sees first.
sections.copyable: Image: [SUBJECT and ACTION]. Setting: [CONTEXT]. Composition: rule of thirds, subject on the right intersection. Camera: [close-up / medium / wide]. Style: [STYLE]. Ratio: 16:9.

sections.heading: Camera language in the prompt (optional)
sections.body: Composition and camera language help you control what the viewer sees first.
sections.copyable: Image: [SUBJECT and ACTION]. Setting: [CONTEXT]. Composition: rule of thirds, subject on the right intersection. Camera: [close-up / medium / wide]. Style: [STYLE]. Ratio: 16:9.

sections.heading: In short
sections.body: Recognise which prompt field needs fixing: style, ratio, composition or brand consistency.

sections.heading: Do this now
sections.body: Recognise which prompt field needs fixing: style, ratio, composition or brand consistency.

recognitionExercise.title: Recognise style and ratios
recognitionExercise.task: Read 5 situations and choose the best answer for each one.
examples: You need a square LinkedIn post with a product image.
The same product should look like a premium catalogue photo.
The hero image needs space for a headline on the left.
All campaign images must use the same colours and tone.
A Stories format needs a vertical frame.
explanations: A square social post uses a 1:1 ratio.
A premium catalogue look is controlled mainly by style: light, texture, realism and tone.
Leaving space for a headline is a composition decision.
Repeating colours and tone protects brand consistency across images.
Stories, Reels and similar mobile formats usually use a vertical 9:16 ratio.
recognitionExercise.goal: Before using the builder, recognise which prompt field needs fixing.

sections.heading: How to use this slide
sections.body: Use one MASTER template or one ready prompt if you need a fast result; use the rest for deeper practice.

sections.heading: AI image workflow (5 steps)
sections.body: (1) Concept – goal, context, style, mood, audience. (2) Prompt formulation – subjects, style, composition, lighting, palette, technical params. (3) Optimise – priority structure, negative prompts, 3–5 test variants. (4) Generate + iterate. (5) Post-processing – upscale, colour, retouch.

sections.heading: #1000Books – book illustrations
sections.body: Sequence: book title + summary → artist role: 5 concepts → pick the best → prompt engineer role: text-to-image prompt → copy into Ideogram/Leonardo. To make it professional, add: evaluation criteria, file naming/versioning (V1/V2) and a rights note.

sections.heading: MASTER prompt template
sections.body: Use one MASTER template or one ready prompt if you need a fast result; use the rest for deeper practice.
sections.copyable: Subject: [what is shown]. Goal: [awareness / engagement / conversion]. Audience: [who]. Style: [style]. Camera: [shot and angle]. Lighting: [lighting]. Colors: [palette]. Format: [1:1 / 16:9 / 9:16]. Avoid: [what to avoid].

sections.heading: Ready prompt: Logotipas
sections.body: Use one MASTER template or one ready prompt if you need a fast result; use the rest for deeper practice.
sections.copyable: Subject: [what is shown]. Goal: [awareness / engagement / conversion]. Audience: [who]. Style: [style]. Camera: [shot and angle]. Lighting: [lighting]. Colors: [palette]. Format: [1:1 / 16:9 / 9:16]. Avoid: [what to avoid].

sections.heading: Ready prompt: Social post
sections.body: Use one MASTER template or one ready prompt if you need a fast result; use the rest for deeper practice.
sections.copyable: Subject: [what is shown]. Goal: [awareness / engagement / conversion]. Audience: [who]. Style: [style]. Camera: [shot and angle]. Lighting: [lighting]. Colors: [palette]. Format: [1:1 / 16:9 / 9:16]. Avoid: [what to avoid].

sections.heading: Ready prompt: Plakatas
sections.body: Use one MASTER template or one ready prompt if you need a fast result; use the rest for deeper practice.
sections.copyable: Subject: [what is shown]. Goal: [awareness / engagement / conversion]. Audience: [who]. Style: [style]. Camera: [shot and angle]. Lighting: [lighting]. Colors: [palette]. Format: [1:1 / 16:9 / 9:16]. Avoid: [what to avoid].

sections.heading: All 8 business scenarios
sections.body: Use one MASTER template or one ready prompt if you need a fast result; use the rest for deeper practice.

sections.heading: Check
sections.body: Use one MASTER template or one ready prompt if you need a fast result; use the rest for deeper practice.

slides.title: Image prompt builder
slides.subtitle: Campaign context, visual and text in one prompt

content.tldr: In short: Fill in the fields below – the system builds the image prompt. Handy after the optional "Workflow and MASTER templates" slide (13.35), where you fill fields by hand. Copy and paste into any image generation tool.
content.patikra: Does the generated prompt include at least 3 elements (subject, style, proportions)? Did the result meet expectations? If not – go back and change the parameters.

content.title: Video generation
content.subtitle: Scenarijaus rasymas trumpiems vaizdo irasams, kadravimas ir formatas. Kodel AI video verta demesio – ir kaip is karto isbandyti (Sora, Runway, Veo 3).

sections.heading: In short
sections.body: Short video: clear script, tone, camera. Better 2–4 short clips (3–5 s) than one 20–30 s one-shot. Lock storyboard stills before expensive video generation (see 13.12).

sections.heading: Framing and camera angle
sections.body: Framing changes emotion: eye level, high angle, low angle, POV. Specify in the prompt. Video models handle motion and time best – not visual content from scratch.

sections.heading: Image → video chain
sections.body: Hero / keyframe → I2V (Runway, Kling, Veo, Seedance, Sora) → edit. Audio-first hint: if there will be VO, lock VO duration first – then cut clips to match audio.

sections.heading: Do this now
sections.body: Write 2–3 sentences for one 3–5 s shot. Copy the template below.

sections.heading: Copyable prompt
sections.body: Single short clip template.
sections.copyable:
Clip 3–5 s (no longer).
Script: [what happens in this frame].
Camera: [slow push-in / side move / stable / crane up].
Tone: [professional / dynamic / calm].
Start: image-to-video from hero keyframe. Same style, same colors.

sections.heading: Copyable chain: image to video
sections.body: Hero first, then animation; for longer – multiple keyframes.
sections.copyable:
1) Create hero image: [SUBJECT], setting [CONTEXT], style [STYLE], ratio 16:9 or 9:16, no text in image.
2) Animation from this image: 3–5 seconds. Camera moves […], subject [action]. Same character/product, same style, same color palette.
3) (If longer needed) Repeat 2) with a second keyframe – edit together later.

sections.heading: Check
sections.body: Does the video start look like the hero? Did product/character drift? If not – simplify the scene or strengthen reference (13.32).

sections.heading: Same look across shots
sections.body: Same reference + “same product / same style”. Avoid real faces/voices without consent (13.101).

sections.heading: Why it is useful and what to specify
sections.body: AI lets you test variants fast. Specify format (16:9 / 9:16) and 3–5 s duration. Track CPI (cost per usable clip) = total generation + retry cost / usable clips – not just €/second. Before publishing – rights.

sections.heading: 2026 video tool matrix
sections.body: Seedance 2.0 – directed motion + many refs. Kling 3.0 – quality/cost balance, I2V. Veo 3.1 – highest quality + native audio. Sora 2 – OpenAI ecosystem, 1–2 image refs. Synthesia – avatars / training. InVideo / CapCut – social templates and editing.

sections.heading: Do this now
sections.body: Pick one tool and generate one 3–5 s I2V clip. Note how many retries it took (CPI note).

sections.heading: Copyable prompt
sections.body: Short clip + CPI note.
sections.copyable:
Video clip from keyframe: [1–2 sentences of action].
Format: [16:9 / 9:16]. Duration: 3–5 sec. Style: [specify].
CPI note: how many retries until usable? [N]

sections.heading: Check
sections.body: File downloaded? Commercial rights OK? What was CPI (retries included)?

sections.heading: All video tools (overview)
sections.body: Seedance, Kling 3, Veo 3.1, Sora 2, Runway, Pika, Luma Dream Machine, Synthesia, InVideo. Full list – Tools section (module 13).

sections.heading: Video prompt fields
sections.body: duration_s (3–5), scene, visual_style, camera_movement, lighting, composition, aspect_ratio, reference_ids, negative, audio, brand_guidelines, safety_flags, cpi_note.

slides.title: Quick check: video prompt and format
slides.shortTitle: Quick check: video prompt and format
slides.subtitle: 3 questions before post-prod and audio

questions.question: What does a short video prompt need?
options: Script, duration (3–5 s), format and tone
Only the music genre
Only a square ratio
Only one narrow detail
questions.explanation: The best answer uses a clear goal, context, format and quality check.

questions.question: When should you choose a 9:16 format?
options: When creating a vertical Stories, Reels or TikTok-style clip
When creating a wide presentation frame
When creating a square LinkedIn post
When creating only an audio fragment
questions.explanation: The best answer uses a clear goal, context, format and quality check.

questions.question: What should you check before publishing generated video publicly?
options: Usage rights, face/voice consent and whether the result fits the brand
Only whether the file has a nice name
Only whether the video is longer than 1 minute
Only whether the prompt was written in English
questions.explanation: The best answer uses a clear goal, context, format and quality check.

slides.title: Post-production
slides.subtitle: AI = raw material; cut, grade, mix

sections.heading: In short
sections.body: AI video = raw material, not the final deliverable. Professional practice: edit 3–5 s clips, color grade, text/overlay, audio mix, export for platform (CapCut / Premiere).

sections.heading: Post-production diagram
sections.body: Four steps – Cut → Grade → Mix → Export. Tap a stage.

sections.heading: Minimum checklist
sections.body: (1) Assemble 2–4 clips per script / VO. (2) Cut weak frames; hook in first 1–2 s. (3) Consistent colours. (4) VO or bed + SFX. (5) Loudness guide ~−14 LUFS (music) / ~−16 (VO mix) – trust your ears. (6) Export 9:16 or 16:9.

sections.heading: Do this now
sections.body: Write a 4-line edit plan for your mini clip.

sections.heading: Copyable template
sections.body: Edit plan 15–30 s.
sections.copyable:
Edit plan (15–30 s):
0–3 s: [hook clip]
3–8 s: [product / benefit]
8–15 s: [proof / detail]
End: [CTA frame + text]
Audio: [VO / bed] | rights: [licensed]

sections.heading: Check
sections.body: Without AI “magic”, does the clip read as a story? Does audio drown VO?

content.title: Audio
content.subtitle: Audio-first: VO or bed duration first, then video. VO / SFX / music triad. Licences: ElevenMusic, Soundraw, Beatoven vs Suno demo.

sections.heading: In short
sections.body: Audio-first: VO (or bed duration) first, then video cuts to match pacing. For music describe mood, style, tempo, instruments. Client / ads – licensed stack (ElevenMusic, Soundraw, Beatoven); Suno/Udio – demo, not client work.

sections.heading: Do this now
sections.body: If you have VO – generate or record VO first. If bed only – copy the music prompt. Mark license intent.

sections.heading: Copyable prompt – bed
sections.body: Background music template.
sections.copyable:
Create a background music fragment, 30–60 seconds.
Mood: [calm / energetic]. Style: [acoustic / electronic / piano].
Tempo: [slow / medium]. No vocals. Use: [ad / presentation] – commercial licence required.

sections.heading: Copyable prompt – VO
sections.body: Voiceover template (ElevenLabs etc.).
sections.copyable:
Voiceover, [EN/LT], tone [professional / friendly], pace [calm].
Script: [paste 2–4 sentences].
No background music in file – voice only. I will mix with bed later.

sections.heading: Check
sections.body: Does bed drown VO? Does the licence allow ads?

sections.heading: English MASTER template (universal)
sections.body: Many music tools understand English field names better. Add license intent.
sections.copyable:
Create a [genre] track,
mood: [emotion],
tempo: [bpm or speed],
instruments: [list],
vocal: none,
structure: intro – bed – soft outro,
target platform: [YouTube/TikTok/ads],
goal: background / branding,
license intent: commercial,
production quality: professional.

sections.heading: TOP audio tools
sections.body: ElevenLabs – VO and SFX. ElevenMusic – licensed music, client work. Soundraw / Beatoven – royalty-friendly beds. Suno / Udio – fast demos, not client. Full list – Tools section.

content.footer: Next – slide 17: Rights and audio effects

sections.heading: In short
sections.body: Separate SFX from music. For commercial work – licensed tools. Loudness guide: ~−14 LUFS (music) / ~−16 (VO mix) – final call by ear. Campaign legal / C2PA – slide 13.101.

sections.heading: Rights checklist (5 points)
sections.body: (1) Terms / License. (2) Does the plan allow commercial? (3) YouTube / ads / podcast? (4) Attribution? (5) Write: “Licence allows / does not allow – [tool, date].”

sections.heading: Do this now
sections.body: Before public use open ToS; note “commercial OK?” yes/no.

sections.heading: Copyable prompt
sections.body: SFX template.
sections.copyable:
Create a short sound: [e.g. “soft transition whoosh, 1 second”].
Format: WAV or MP3. No music – SFX only.

sections.heading: Check
sections.body: Download OK? Licence OK for ads / YouTube?

sections.heading: Tools
sections.body: ElevenLabs (VO/SFX), ElevenMusic, Soundraw, Beatoven, Suno, Udio, Boomy. Full list – Tools section (module 13).

slides.title: Business and risks
slides.subtitle: Metrics, A/B, rights, QA and versions

sections.heading: In short
sections.body: Before publishing campaign visuals: measure results, test variants and assess rights and risk. Details are in the expandable blocks below.

sections.heading: Rights and risks (required)
sections.body: Before publishing check: (1) copyright and commercial licence; (2) deepfake – face/voice only with consent; (3) trademarks; (4) GDPR; (5) provenance – C2PA Content Credentials, SynthID/watermark, human-visible AI label (EU AI Act Art. 50). Social often strips metadata – Soft Binding / watermark helps.

sections.heading: Metrics and A/B testing
sections.body: Track KPIs – CTR, CVR, CPM (see glossary), scroll stop. A/B test with 3–5 variants and a clear hypothesis.

sections.heading: A/B hypothesis
sections.body: Copy and fill the hypothesis template below.
sections.copyable:
Test: [A vs B – e.g. "Image A: person using product / Image B: product only on white"].
Hypothesis: [e.g. "Variant A will get higher CTR because it shows usage"].
Metric: CTR (or CVR, scroll stop). Duration: [e.g. 7 days]. Audience: [who sees it].

sections.heading: Rights, risks and business (more)
sections.body:
Deeper dive: copyright, deepfake, trademarks, GDPR. Provenance: C2PA manifest (who/which model), persistent watermark (e.g. SynthID), human-visible label. Checklist: does the tool mark output? disclosure line in brief? “This visual / video was created with AI; tool: [X].”

Use cases: A/B, product visuals, social. Avoid unsourced percentages.

sections.heading: Before publishing: QA and versions
sections.body:
Check: (1) Text in image. (2) Brand. (3) Format. (4) Message. (5) Brand safety. (6) Rights. (7) Disclosure / C2PA / watermark. (8) Reference lock.

Files: V1, V2, V3 + what changed in the prompt.

sections.heading: Evaluation rubric
sections.body: Before publishing, measure results, test variants and check rights, brand safety and versions.
sections.copyable: Evaluate this artefact by 3 criteria: brand fit, message clarity, platform fit. Context: [goal, audience, platform]. Artefact: [describe or paste prompt]. Return: criterion, score 1-5, what works, 1-2 specific fixes.

sections.heading: Top 3 pitfalls
sections.body: (1) Over-abstract prompts → be specific + MASTER. (2) Text in visuals without control → Ideogram/GPT-Image + typography. (3) Long video one-shot → 3–5 s clips + edit; track CPI.

sections.heading: Workflow diagram
sections.body: Move from brief to prompt, variants, iteration, platform adaptation, testing and optimisation.

sections.heading: In short
sections.body: Full business cycle: (1) Marketing brief. (2) Prompt + brand + reference lock. (3) 3–5 variants / short I2V. (4) Iteration (CPI video). (5) Platform adaptation. (6) A/B. (7) Optimisation + disclosure. For the technical media pipeline see MUST slide “Generative media pipeline” (13.12); optional MASTER – 13.35. Parallel team work only after shared brief, brand and (if needed) VO duration.

sections.heading: Do this now
sections.body: Move from brief to prompt, variants, iteration, platform adaptation, testing and optimisation.

sections.heading: Brief into prompt
sections.body: Move from brief to prompt, variants, iteration, platform adaptation, testing and optimisation.
sections.copyable: Brief: Goal [awareness / engagement / conversion]. Audience: [describe]. Platform: [e.g. Instagram 1:1]. Image prompt: [subject] + [action/context] + [setting]. Brand: [colors and tone]. Variants: 3-5.

sections.heading: Check
sections.body: Move from brief to prompt, variants, iteration, platform adaptation, testing and optimisation.

sections.heading: Platform, funnel and copy together
sections.body: Move from brief to prompt, variants, iteration, platform adaptation, testing and optimisation.

content.reflectionPrompt:
META: You are a training reflection assistant. Goal – consolidate what you learned after Module 13.
INPUT: I just finished training on content engineering – image, video, and music prompts with AI, style, aspect ratios, and usage rights.
OUTPUT: Ask 3 questions: (1) Which content type could I apply today? (2) What was newest? (3) What do I want to try first? After my answers, give one concrete tip.
content.tagline: Image + video + music = one content engineering path.
content.nextStepCTA: Open the modules list or repeat the project with another topic
content.firstAction24h: You learned the core image, video and music prompt patterns.

modules.title: Knowledge check: Content path
modules.subtitle: Test: images, video, audio
modules.description: Check image, video, and audio principles. ≥70% recommended before Module 15.

content.whyBenefit: After this test you will know whether you are ready for the final Content engineering project (Module 15).
content.firstActionCTA: Answer 12 questions – images, video, audio, pipeline, audio-first, licences, C2PA, risks and workflow.
content.microWinPhrase: Each correct answer shows that you can apply content prompts.
content.thresholdExplanation: At 70% or more, continue to Module 15. Below 70%, review the recommended Module 13 slides.

testQuestions.question: What should a good image prompt include?
options: Only one narrow detail
Subject, style, ratio and what to avoid
Only one narrow detail
Only one narrow detail
testQuestions.explanation: The best answer uses a clear goal, context, format and quality check.

testQuestions.scenarioContext: You need a LinkedIn post series: every image should look like one brand, and Stories must be vertical.
testQuestions.question: What must you put in the prompt to reduce chaotic brand results and hit the right format?
options: Only the word "nice" and any tool name
Brand colours, tone, visual identity and ratios (e.g. 1:1 feed / 9:16 Stories)
Only the file name and duration in seconds
Only the music BPM
testQuestions.explanation: Brand consistency (colours, tone, identity) plus clear ratios per platform keeps the model from inventing a random style.

testQuestions.question: What should you specify for a short video?
options: Only one narrow detail
A script: what happens, duration and tone
Only one narrow detail
Only one narrow detail
testQuestions.explanation: The best answer uses a clear goal, context, format and quality check.

testQuestions.question: What should you describe for music generation?
options: Only one narrow detail
Mood, style, tempo and, if needed, instruments or voice
Only one narrow detail
Only one narrow detail
testQuestions.explanation: The best answer uses a clear goal, context, format and quality check.

testQuestions.scenarioContext: You are planning a public campaign where an AI-made face or voice looks or sounds like a real person.
testQuestions.question: What should you check before publishing?
options: Only image sharpness
Consent, rights and deepfake risk
Only the color palette
Only the social media algorithm
testQuestions.explanation: Faces and voices in public campaigns raise ethics and legal questions: consent, likeness rights, tool rules and deepfake risk.

testQuestions.question: In the full brief-to-publication workflow, what comes right after the brief?
options: A clear, specific option based on goal, format and checks
Write a brand-aligned prompt and plan several variants
A clear, specific option based on goal, format and checks
A clear, specific option based on goal, format and checks
testQuestions.explanation: The best answer uses a clear goal, context, format and quality check.

testQuestions.scenarioContext: Your goal is a conversion ad for Instagram Stories. You need a hero image with a clear product and space for a headline.
testQuestions.question: Which prompt best fits this situation?
options: Create a beautiful image that catches people's attention.

Create a vertical 9:16 product hero image: [product] in the foreground, clean background, brand colors [X], space for a headline at the top, clear call-to-action area at the bottom, no random text inside the image.
Create any 1:1 image with many details and a lot of text.
Create an artistic image without the product; only the mood matters.
testQuestions.explanation: A conversion visual needs a clear format, product, brand rules and space for text. Pure emotion or abstract beauty fits awareness better.

testQuestions.scenarioContext: You want a short 5–8 second clip that keeps the same product look and colors as the campaign hero visual.
testQuestions.question: What is the safest workflow here?
options: Write only a broad text-to-video prompt and hope the style matches.
Generate the hero image / keyframe first, then use it in an image-to-video tool and ask it to keep the same style and colors.
Start from the music and create the video without a script.
Generate a 1-minute video with one prompt, because longer is always better.
testQuestions.explanation: The image → video chain helps preserve the same style, object and colors. A long video in one prompt is safer as short, clear scenes.

testQuestions.question: Why have storyboard / stills before expensive video generation?
options: So the file is larger
To lock composition and reduce retries / CPI
So you can skip the brief
So you can skip rights checks
testQuestions.explanation: Stills / storyboard lock the visual cheaply; the video model then handles motion. That cuts randomness and cost per usable clip (CPI).

testQuestions.question: What does audio-first workflow mean?
options: Video first, then any audio
Finish VO (or bed duration) first, then cut / generate video to match audio pacing
SFX only, no music
Silent export only
testQuestions.explanation: Audio-first: VO or bed sets edit rhythm. If audio “floats”, video feels disconnected.

testQuestions.scenarioContext: A client needs background music for a monetised YouTube ad.
testQuestions.question: Which choice should you prioritise?
options: Suno free demo without checking licence
Licensed stack (ElevenMusic, Soundraw or Beatoven) and ToS check
Any random file from the internet
Silence, because music is never allowed
testQuestions.explanation: Client / ad / monetisation – licensed tools. Suno/Udio suit demos, not “probably nobody will notice”.

testQuestions.question: What is C2PA / disclosure practice before publishing AI content?
options: Only a nice file name
Provenance marking: Content Credentials / watermark and, where required, human-visible AI label
Only colour correction
Only a longer prompt
testQuestions.explanation: EU AI Act Art. 50 and platform policies require recognisable synthetic content: C2PA, watermark (e.g. SynthID), human-visible label.

content.passedMessage: Great work! You can continue to Module 15: Content engineering project.
content.failedMessage: We recommend reviewing Module 13 slides again – pipeline, images, video, audio.
content.thresholdExplanation: At 70% or more, continue to Module 15. Below 70%, review the recommended Module 13 slides.

useCaseBlock.heading: Next step: Module 15
useCaseBlock.body: Prepare at least a hero image (150.5); if you want – a mini campaign: video, audio and edit (151–154). Tools – Tools section.

content.reflectionPrompt:
META: You are a training reflection assistant. Goal – consolidate knowledge after the Content path test.
INPUT: I just finished Module 14 – images, video, audio, pipeline, audio-first, C2PA.
OUTPUT: Ask 3 questions: (1) Which artefact will I create in Module 15? (2) What was newest? (3) What do I want to try first? After my answers, give one concrete tip.

modules.title: Final project: Content path
modules.subtitle: Quick start or mini campaign
modules.description: Required: a main hero image with your prompt. Optionally add video, audio, and edit.

slides.title: Content engineering project
slides.subtitle: Required: hero image; optional: video, audio, edit

content.whyBenefit: After the project you will have a hero image with the prompt you used and a brief; optionally a mini campaign pack (video + audio + edit) and templates for later work.
content.duration: ~20 min quick start / ~60–90 min full path
content.firstActionCTA: Start with the quick path: create one hero image with the prompt you used. For the full path – continue into video, audio and edit.
content.recommendedStart: After you choose: quick path ends at the hero image (150.5); full path continues 151–154.
content.primaryPathIntro: Required minimum: hero image (150.5). After the quick start you can go to the summary.
content.taskOneLiner: Complete 150.5 to finish the module; 151–154 – optional full mini campaign path.

full.label: Full path
full.description: ~60–90 min – mini campaign: image → video → audio → edit.

content.scenarioDescription: In about 20 minutes create one hero image, the prompt you used and a 2-line brief. This is the required minimum to complete M15.

scenario.narrativeLead: Write a short brief: goal, audience, platform. Then generate one hero image and save the prompt.

content.template: Brief: goal [awareness / engagement / conversion], audience [who], platform [where]. Create a hero image: [subject and action], setting [context], style [style], ratio [1:1 / 16:9 / 9:16]. Brand: colors [X], tone [professional / friendly / premium]. No text in the image unless needed.

steps.title: Generate the hero image and save the prompt

slides.title: Project loop: branch and iterate
slides.subtitle: Quick start or optional full path

sections.heading: In short
sections.body: Quick path: brief → one hero image → prompt → tweak → summary. Full path (optional): hero → 3–5 s I2V → VO/bed → 15–30 s edit → QA + disclosure.
sections.imageAlt: Content project loop: quick path and full mini campaign path

sections.heading: Do now
sections.body: Name your path: quick (150.5 only) or full (151–154). Write your first artefact in one sentence.

sections.heading: Check
sections.body: Is the first artefact clear (hero image + prompt + brief)? Do you know the next step – summary (quick) or 151 (full)?

slides.title: Checkpoint: project path
slides.subtitle: Content path – confirm your path before scenarios

content.title: Is your project path clear?
content.body: Before continuing, confirm your path. If 150.5 is done and you choose the quick path – go to the summary. If you want a mini campaign – continue to 151–154.

sections.heading: Quick path (required minimum)
sections.body: One **hero image** with the prompt you used and a 2-line brief (slide 150.5). That is enough to complete M15 – then **go to the summary**.

sections.heading: Full path (optional)
sections.body: Hero → **video** (3–5 s) → **audio** (VO/bed) → **edit** (154) → QA. Mini campaign pack (slides 151–154).

sections.heading: Check
sections.body: Which path do you choose – quick (summary) or full (151)? What is your first artefact?

slides.title: Scenario: Image
slides.subtitle: Optional: keyframe for the full path

content.scenarioDescription: Optional. If you already have the 150.5 hero image – you can skip to the summary, or reuse it as a keyframe. Otherwise create a hero image for your topic. Artefact: image + prompt.

scenario.narrativeLead: If 150.5 is done – skip or reuse that hero as the keyframe for 152. This image will be the start frame for video generation.

content.template: Hero image / keyframe: [WHAT IS SHOWN]. Setting: [CONTEXT]. Style: [STYLE]. Brand colors: [X]. Ratio: [1:1 / 16:9 / 9:16]. Leave space for a headline if needed.
content.templateLabel: Prompt – copy into an image tool

steps.title: Generate hero image / keyframe (or skip)
steps.description: If the quick path is done – go to the summary. Otherwise: image + prompt → 152.

content.scenarioDescription: Optional: use 151 (or 150.5) as keyframe and generate a 3–5 s clip (or 2 clips). Artefact: video / link + prompt + CPI note.

scenario.narrativeLead: Use a Module 13 video prompt template and the hero image as the start frame. Goal – keep the same style, colors and subject.

content.template: Animation from hero image: 3–5 sec. Script: [DESCRIBE]. Camera moves [slow push-in / side move / stable]. Tone: [professional / dynamic / calm]. Format: [16:9 / 9:16]. Same style, same colors. CPI note: retries [N].
content.templateLabel: Prompt – copy into a video tool

steps.title: Generate video from the hero image
steps.description: Artefact: video or link + prompt. Check that the style did not drift.

slides.title: Scenario: Audio (VO or bed)
slides.subtitle: Optional: VO or 30–60 s bed + rights

content.scenarioDescription: Optional: generate VO or 30–60 s background music. Artefact: file / link + prompt + “commercial OK?”.

scenario.narrativeLead: Use a Module 13 music prompt template and match the mood to the hero image and video. Before use, note whether the licence allows public use.

content.template:
Option A (bed): 30–60 sec, mood […], no vocals, license intent: commercial.
Option B (VO): [2–4 sentences], tone […], voice only.
content.templateLabel: Prompt – copy into a music tool

steps.title: Generate music or sound and save the prompt
steps.description: Artefact: file or link + prompt + one sentence on usage rights.

content.scenarioDescription: Optional: assemble a 15–30 s final clip from 2–4 I2V shots + VO or bed. Artefact: export / link + checklist (rights, disclosure, CPI).

scenario.narrativeLead: Use CapCut or similar. AI = raw. Hook in first 1–2 s. Mark disclosure and rights.

content.template:
Edit plan (15–30 s):
0–3 s: [hook]
3–8 s: [product / benefit]
8–15 s: [detail]
End: [CTA]
Audio: [VO / bed] | rights: [licensed]
Disclosure: AI-created with [tool]. CPI note: [N retries].

steps.title: Edit and export
steps.description: Artefact: file / link + rights + disclosure + CPI note.

content.introBody: Congratulations! You finished the quick start or full mini campaign path. You have an artefact, a prompt and a clearer path: hero → video → audio → edit.

sections.heading: Image
items: Hero image / keyframe with brand colours
Artefact: image + prompt used + V1/V2 note if you iterated

sections.heading: Trumpas vaizdo irasas
items: Create, check and reuse the artefact with a clear prompt.
Create, check and reuse the artefact with a clear prompt.
Create, check and reuse the artefact with a clear prompt.

sections.heading: Audio, edit and checklist
items: Checklist: brief, prompt, refs?, disclosure?, CPI?

sections.heading: Next step
items: Return to the modules list – repeat practice with another topic or open another advanced path (data analysis 7–9, agents 10–12).
Save your best prompts – use them as templates.

content.reflectionPrompt: Ask me 3 reflection questions: (1) Which artefact or prompt will I use in 24 hours? (2) What would I improve in the next version? (3) Did I write down usage rights? Then give one practical suggestion.
content.tagline: Image + video + audio = one content engineering path.
content.nextStepCTA: Open the modules list or repeat the project with another topic
content.firstAction24h: Within 24–48 hours use your quick start hero image or one mini campaign element in a real project (social post, slide or background).
